title: Ophiopogon griffithii
taxonomy: Ophiopogon griffithii is classified in the family Asparagaceae, specifically in the subfamily Agavoideae and the genus Ophiopogon. This plant belongs to the kingdom Plantae and the division Magnoliophyta. Further classification places it in the class Liliopsida and the order Asparagales. The family Asparagaceae is a large and diverse group of flowering plants, including many species of grasses and lilies. Within the genus Ophiopogon, Ophiopogon griffithii is one of many species of flowering plants native to Asia.

intro: Ophiopogon griffithii, also known as Griffith's Mondo Grass, is a type of slow-growing, perennial plant native to the mountainous regions of Asia. This plant has been used in traditional Chinese medicine for its purported health benefits, which include reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and treating respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma. The bioactive constituents of Ophiopogon griffithii include steroidal saponins, particularly griffithii saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Ophiopogon griffithii is often prepared as a decoction, capsule, or tablet, and is also used in combination with other herbs to enhance its therapeutic effects.
